content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=One-line taxonomy of v3 memory components summary=Section 8 provides a compact role map for core memory types: Source (observed/said data), Entity (identity anchor), Note (useful context not yet hardened), Claim (normalized fact), Profile (stable context projection), Task (required action), Episode (learned lesson), and MemoryRun (how memory was updated). scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1>,entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6>,entity:<7>,entity:<8> keywords=v3 memory taxonomy,one-line model,Source Entity Note Claim tags=doc_digest,data_model,memory_types evidence=- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено - `Entity` = кто/что это вообще - `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт - `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом - `Profile` = компакт...[truncated 152 chars]

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 data model uses layered memory architecture with two cross-cutting concerns summary=In `v3`, the data model is intentionally split into layers (`Source`, `Entity`/`Claim`, `Note`, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode`, `MemoryRun`) to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability; `scope` and `retrieval lifecycle` are defined as cross-cutting concerns. scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3 data model,layered memory architecture,scope,retrieval lifecycle tags=data_model,architecture,memory_layers,cross_cutting useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim vs Note have complementary roles and should not be collapsed summary=In section 4 of `02. Data Model`, `Claim` is for precise factual operations (factual answers, predicate-rule reconciliation, profile updates, and time-aware status answers), while `Note` is for richer contextual memory (decision history, rationale, evolving meaning, later consolidation). The document warns that storing everything as `Claim` creates false precision, and storing everything as `Note`...[truncated 23 chars] scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim`' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Claim vs Note,false precision,richer retrieval tags=data_model,memory_roles,doc_section_digest useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` is an intermediate semantic memory layer with lifecycle and linkage summary=`Note` is explicitly positioned between raw sources and hard claims: a single self-contained semantic fragment for retrieval-worthy context (decisions, rationale, plans, hypotheses, lessons, digests) with status/retrieval/maturity lifecycle, evidence linkage, entity linkage, and note-to-note relations. scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Note,semantic fragment,retrieval_state,maturity_state,note_entities,note_sources,note_links tags=data_model,memory_v3,note_design useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended schema/Pydantic field semantics for Note, Claim, Task, and Profile summary=Section 7 provides recommended semantic meanings for key fields: `Note.summary` should be self-contained and uncertainty-aware; `scope_json` constrains applicability; `Note.maturity_score` reflects stability; `Claim.normalized_text` is stand-alone factual text; `Claim.retrieval_state` is search temperature; `Task.acceptance_criteria_json` defines observable completion; `Profile.soft_signals` shoul...[truncated 34 chars] scope=Applies to section 7 'Recommended field descriptions для schema / Pydantic' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Pydantic,schema,summary,scope_json,maturity_score,normalized_text,retrieval_state,acceptance_criteria_json,soft_signals tags=schema_guidance,field_semantics,memory_model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`PredicateCatalog` defines reconciliation behavior per predicate summary=In the v3 memory model, `PredicateCatalog` is a configuration component (not a memory item) that controls how each predicate is reconciled, including cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and whether facts project into profile/task layers. scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy,profile_sync,task_sync tags=data_model,memory_v3,schema_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` as canonical memory anchor with reversible merge policy summary=`Entity` is the canonical memory node used to unify mentions and anchor claims/tasks/notes/episodes, with alias and merge support. Merge must preserve history, aliases must resolve to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges must be reversible via audit trail. scope=Applies to section 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,entity_resolution,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 retention policy defines lifecycle tiers and deletion controls summary=Section 6 requires an explicit retention policy in v3: low-use notes can move to cold storage, old resolved items to archived, sensitive source data supports soft/hard deletion on request, and historical claims may remain explainable while being deprioritized for top retrieval. scope=Applies to section 6 'Retention / forgetting' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=retention policy,cold,archived,soft delete,hard delete,top retrieval tags=data_model,retention,lifecycle useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Task entity usage rules and lifecycle invariants summary=In section 3.7, `Task` is operational memory for future actions and should be created only when real future action is required. Core invariants are: plain questions are not tasks, requests are not commitments, each task must represent one clear commitment, and status should be updated instead of creating duplicates; evidence linkage is handled via separate `task_sources` with `support_type` (`dire...[truncated 36 chars] scope=Applies to section 3.7 `Task` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Task,task_sources,task invariants,status update,commitment tags=data_model,task,invariants,evidence_linkage useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<8> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Claim` schema role and quality invariants in v3 summary=In `02. Data Model` section 3.5, `Claim` is the atomic fact/relation unit for precise factual answers, predicate-based reconciliation, and explainable retrieval; each claim should represent one predicate application and remain self-contained, with explicit statements preferred over inference and weak/soft meaning redirected to `Note` instead of low-quality claims. scope=Applies to section 3.5 `Claim` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Claim,predicate-based reconciliation,claim_sources,invariants tags=data_model,v3,claim,schema_invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<9> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=MemoryRun recommended schema and invariants for idempotent, durable run logging summary=The section lists recommended `MemoryRun` fields (including `run_type`, `trigger_mode`, retrieval/prompt/model/IO/ops/status/error metrics) and states two invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` supports idempotency and retry safety. scope=Applies to section 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=MemoryRun fields,run_type,trigger_mode,input_hash,retry safety tags=schema,invariants,memoryrun useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<10> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Episode is post-MVP optional memory for reusable lessons summary=Section 3.8 defines `Episode` as an optional memory type intended for later phases (not MVP) to preserve transferable experience such as successful tactics, failure patterns, and reusable lessons. Episodes must avoid raw chain-of-thought and store lesson-oriented outcomes rather than full reasoning traces. scope=Applies to section 3.8 `Episode` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Episode,MVP,lessons learned,failure patterns,chain-of-thought tags=data_model,episode,roadmap,policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<11> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=MemoryRun is the audit/debug run log for pipeline observability summary=Section 3.9 defines `MemoryRun` as an audit trail and debug layer to track prompt/model usage, retrieved items, budget, proposed vs applied operations, and pipeline failure points for each run. scope=Applies to section 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=MemoryRun,audit trail,debug layer,pipeline observability tags=memory_model,doc_digest,memoryrun useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<12> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Profile` as compact rebuildable projection layer summary=Section 3.6 defines `Profile` as a short, always-in-context projection (not source of truth) built from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should prioritize explicit facts over inference, keep disputed facts in `Claim`/`Note`, and remain rebuildable. scope=Applies to section 3.6 `Profile` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Profile,projection layer,always-in-context,rebuildable tags=data_model,v3,profile,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<13> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Source` component role and invariants in v3 memory model summary=`Source` is the raw evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records and supports re-check and explainability. It should be immutable in practice, keep `observed_at` separate from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce explicit delete policy. scope=Applies to section 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,memory_architecture,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<14> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note maturity lifecycle and promotion/degradation signals summary=Section 5 defines a required maturity model for `Note`: `fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`. Maturity should increase when a note is used in retrieval, supported by multiple sources, survives without supersede, and answers recurring rationale questions; it should decrease/reset on contradiction, supersede, scope drift, or long-term disuse. scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 5. Note maturity' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Note maturity,fresh stabilizing mature consolidated,promotion signals tags=note_lifecycle,maturity_model,retrieval_quality useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.87,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Captures the dedicated one-line definitions for all core memory objects in section 8; related to but more specific than the existing layered-architecture summary.","target_note_id":null}]}
